concept.title: Elements cookbook for HTML customization
concept.shortdesc: This topic intentionally uses a wide range of common DITA elements so you can validate your HTML5 XSLT overrides and CSS hooks across real structures.

section.title: Phrasing content: ph, keyword-like spans, and inline semantics
section.p: Use ph for generic phrasing content. For styling, use attributes like outputclass to add stable CSS hooks.
Example: BETA label and Ctrl+C shortcut.
Novice note: focus on a minimal set of elements first (p, note, xref, codeblock, table) and expand later.
Admin note: keep a changelog for your customization plug-in and test upgrades in CI.

ul.li: List item with inline ph and a link.

List item with nested list:
First nested step
Second nested step (web-only)
Second nested step (print-only)

dlentry.dd: DITA Open Toolkit. A publishing engine that transforms DITA to outputs like HTML5 and PDF (via plug-ins).
A package that extends DITA-OT with transforms, parameters, templates, and resources.

section.title: Draft comments (draft-comment)
section.p: Draft comments are commonly filtered out in production but are useful during review.
section.draft-comment: TODO: Confirm whether we want to show step labels by default in HTML output.

section.title: Hazard statements (hazardstatement)
section.p: Hazard statements are used heavily in safety-critical documentation. They may be filtered or styled prominently.

hazardstatement.consequence: Electric shock can cause serious injury or death.
hazardstatement.howtoavoid: Disconnect power before servicing and verify with a meter.

image.alt: Placeholder diagram for testing image rendering.
